source_rationale: RGS5 is a canonical marker of pericytes and vascular mural cells, highly expressed in perivascular cells
ACTA2 (alpha smooth muscle actin) is a key marker of pericytes and smooth muscle cells
Transgelin is a smooth muscle-specific protein marking pericytes and vascular smooth muscle cells
Smooth muscle myosin heavy chain is a definitive marker of smooth muscle/pericyte lineage
NOTCH3 is a key signaling receptor specifically expressed in pericytes and vascular mural cells
PDGFRB is a classical pericyte marker essential for pericyte recruitment and function
MCAM (CD146) is a cell adhesion molecule highly expressed on pericytes
Phospholamban is expressed in smooth muscle/pericyte cells regulating calcium signaling
Myosin light chain 9 is a smooth muscle contractile protein marking pericytes
CD3D is a core component of the T cell receptor complex, definitively marking T cells
CD8A marks cytotoxic T lymphocytes, indicating this is a CD8+ T cell cluster
Granzyme K is characteristic of effector memory CD8+ T cells
CCL5 (RANTES) is highly expressed by activated/effector T cells
NKG7 is a cytotoxic granule protein expressed in activated cytotoxic T and NK cells
Granzyme A is a serine protease in cytotoxic granules of CD8+ T cells
CD8B confirms the CD8+ T cell identity of this cluster
Interferon gamma is a hallmark cytokine of activated effector T cells
PTPRC (CD45) is a pan-leukocyte marker confirming immune cell identity
IL7R marks memory T cell populations and is important for T cell homeostasis
Decorin is a proteoglycan abundantly expressed by fibroblasts and stromal cells
PDGFRA is a canonical marker of fibroblasts and mesenchymal stromal cells
Collagen type I alpha 1 is a major extracellular matrix gene produced by fibroblasts
Collagen type I alpha 2 is a fibroblast-produced structural collagen
SERPINF1 (PEDF) is secreted by fibroblasts and stromal cells
Complement C1s is produced by fibroblasts in the ovarian/tissue stroma
MMP2 is a matrix metalloproteinase characteristic of fibroblast extracellular matrix remodeling
Complement factor D (adipsin) is expressed by stromal/fibroblast cells
Osteoglycin is a small leucine-rich proteoglycan expressed by stromal fibroblasts
Stathmin expression suggests this fibroblast population may include proliferating cells
Von Willebrand factor is a definitive endothelial cell marker involved in hemostasis
Claudin 5 is a tight junction protein specific to vascular endothelial cells
CD34 is a classical marker of vascular endothelial cells and hematopoietic progenitors
Endomucin is a transmembrane glycoprotein specific to vascular endothelial cells
EGFL7 is an endothelial-specific secreted factor important for vascular development
CLEC14A is an endothelial-specific C-type lectin involved in angiogenesis
PTPRB (VE-PTP) is an endothelial-specific receptor tyrosine phosphatase
ROBO4 is an endothelial-specific guidance receptor important for vascular integrity
TIE1 is an endothelial receptor tyrosine kinase essential for vascular development
Aquaporin 1 is highly expressed in endothelial cells for water transport
Anti-Müllerian hormone is produced by granulosa cells of ovarian follicles
FOXL2 is a master transcription factor for ovarian granulosa cell identity
Inhibin alpha is a hormone produced by granulosa cells of the ovary
17-beta hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase 1 is a steroidogenic enzyme in granulosa cells
Keratin 18 is an epithelial marker expressed by granulosa cells
WFDC2 (HE4) is expressed in ovarian epithelial/granulosa cells
Desmoplakin marks epithelial cell-cell junctions in granulosa cells
WNT6 signaling is active in ovarian granulosa cell development
Prostaglandin D synthase is highly expressed in granulosa cells of growing follicles
Neuropeptide Y is expressed in ovarian granulosa cells and involved in follicular function
MHC class II expression indicates antigen-presenting immune cells
Complement C1q subunit marks tissue-resident macrophages
CD14 is a monocyte/macrophage lineage marker
TYROBP (DAP12) is an adaptor protein in myeloid/NK cell signaling
Lysozyme is a classical marker of monocytes and macrophages
AIF1 (IBA1) is a macrophage/microglia-specific marker
Fc epsilon receptor gamma chain is expressed on myeloid cells
CCL5 co-expression with myeloid markers suggests activated macrophages with T cell interaction
FCER1A marks dendritic cells and mast cells, suggesting a mixed myeloid/DC population
Co-expression of T cell markers with myeloid genes suggests a mixed immune cluster or doublets
CD163 is a scavenger receptor specific to tissue-resident macrophages
Complement C1q is a hallmark of tissue-resident macrophages
C1QB complement component confirms tissue-resident macrophage identity
Folate receptor 2 marks tissue-resident anti-inflammatory macrophages
LYVE1 is a marker of perivascular tissue-resident macrophages
CD14 is a monocyte/macrophage lineage marker
CSF1R (CD115) is the macrophage colony-stimulating factor receptor essential for macrophage differentiation
Mannose receptor family member marks M2-like tissue-resident macrophages
Stabilin-1 is expressed by tissue-resident macrophages with scavenging function
Coagulation factor XIII A chain marks tissue-resident macrophages
MS4A7 is preferentially expressed in monocytes and macrophages
Zona pellucida glycoprotein 3 is an oocyte-specific gene essential for fertilization
FIGLA is an oocyte-specific transcription factor essential for folliculogenesis
NLRP5 is a maternal-effect gene specifically expressed in oocytes
DPPA3 (Stella) is a germ cell-specific gene marking oocytes
ZAR1 is an oocyte-specific RNA-binding protein essential for early embryogenesis
OOSP2 is an oocyte-secreted protein specific to female germ cells
LHX8 is a transcription factor essential for oocyte development and survival
SOHLH2 is a germ cell-specific transcription factor required for oogenesis
TUBB8 is an oocyte-specific beta-tubulin essential for meiotic spindle assembly
KPNA7 is an oocyte-specific importin required for early development
STAG3 is a meiosis-specific cohesin subunit essential for oocyte chromosome segregation
GTSF1 is involved in piRNA pathway and transposon silencing in germ cells
RGS5 is the top marker, indicating pericytes/vascular mural cells similar to Cluster 1
Alpha smooth muscle actin marks pericytes and smooth muscle cells
Transgelin is a smooth muscle/pericyte contractile protein
Smooth muscle myosin heavy chain confirms smooth muscle/pericyte identity
NOTCH3 is a pericyte-specific signaling receptor
PDGFRB is a canonical pericyte marker
MCAM (CD146) marks pericytes in the vasculature
Phospholamban is expressed in smooth muscle/pericyte cells
Myosin light chain 9 is a contractile protein of smooth muscle/pericytes
Gap junction alpha-4 (connexin 37) is expressed in vascular mural cells
Decorin is a hallmark proteoglycan of fibroblasts and stromal cells
PDGFRA is a canonical fibroblast/stromal cell marker
Collagen type I alpha 1 is a major fibroblast-produced extracellular matrix component
Osteoglycin is a proteoglycan expressed by stromal fibroblasts
TCF21 is a transcription factor marking ovarian theca/stromal fibroblasts
StAR (steroidogenic acute regulatory protein) marks steroidogenic theca/stromal cells in the ovary
Lumican is a proteoglycan characteristic of fibroblasts and connective tissue
Collagen type I alpha 2 confirms fibroblast/stromal identity
APOE is expressed by theca/interstitial stromal cells in the ovary
WISP2 is expressed in ovarian stromal/theca cells involved in tissue remodeling
